channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: MotionRec for PS5, Switch launches April 2
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Switch

Puzzle action game currently available for PC.

The PlayStation 5 and Switch versions of puzzle action game MotionRec will launch on April 2, publisher PLAYISM and developer HANDSUM announced.

MotionRec first launched for PC via Steam on October 27, 2025.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

MotionRec is a recording puzzle action game where you progress through stages by recording and playing back your own movements.

Rec, The Civilization-Recording Robot

MotionRec is a recording-based puzzle action game built around the concept of recording and playback. Use this ability to guide Rec, the civilization-recording robot, through a devastated world.

The Power of MotionRec

You can record the trajectory of your movements, from walking to jumping. Use stage gimmicks to create and record more complex movements to traverse the world.

Record your movements…

…and play them back in another location!

This will let you reach places too high to jump up to, or even access spots where there’s nothing to stand on. You can only play back your movements a certain number of times midair, however, and this is refreshed every time you land on the ground.

Make good use of the MotionRec ability to unravel the mysteries behind this devastated world ruled by machines!

Staff

  • Studio: HANDSUM inc.
  • Pixel Art: m7kenji
  • Music: Kyohei Fujita
  • Developer: shoma

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer

English

Japanese

Simplified Chinese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/motionrec-for-ps5-switch-launches-april-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Laid-Back Camp: Camping Cook-Off! launches March 18 for PC, later for Switch and mobile
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Smartphone
  • Switch

Japanese-only at launch. English language support coming at a later date.

“Camping action game” Laid-Back Camp: Camping Cook-Off! will launch for PC via Steam on March 18 for 3,480 yen, followed by Switch and mobile at a later date, enish announced. The game will only support Japanese at launch, but will gradually add support for additional languages, including English and Traditional Chinese.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Laid-Back Camp: Camping Cook-Off! is a cooperative camping action game for one-to-four players, set in the world of the popular TV anime Laid-Back Camp.

Players can take control of familiar characters such as Nadeshiko and Rin, and tackle a variety of camping tasks including cooking, setting up tents, and preparing campfires. Each stage offers unique gimmicks and challenges, requiring you to work efficiently within a time limit while enjoying the excitement of cooperating with friends.

Key Features

  • One-to-Four-Player Support – Experience the satisfaction of careful planning in solo play, or the fun chaos of cooperation in multiplayer.
  • Story Mode – In addition to original game episodes, you can also experience memorable scenes from the anime.
  • Varied Camping Actions – Cook meals, pitch tents, prepare campfires, and enjoy an authentic outdoor experience.
  • Scoring System – Get evaluated based on time, steps, and teamwork. Challenge yourself repeatedly to sharpen your skills.
  • Planned Downloadable Content – Additional stages, outfits, and more long-term content are on the way.

Cast

The main characters are voiced by the same talented cast from the anime, bringing the atmosphere of the series directly into the game.

Blending the laid-back and heartwarming camping experience of Laid-Back Camp with chaotic cooperative action, this is a new style of multiplayer fun. Recommended not only for anime fans but also for anyone looking for a lively and engaging multiplayer game.

Spend the ultimate camping time with your friends and family!



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/laid-back-camp-camping-cook-off-launches-march-18-for-pc-later-for-switch-and-mobile
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales details Age of Reconstruction
item.content:encoded:
Get the details below.

■ Story

A Story of Four Ages across a Thousand Years

To lift the curse on Princess Heuria of Huther, Elliot and Faie will cross through the Doorway of Time to visit different periods in history.

They will travel from their own time, the Age of Safekeeping, further and further into the past. Across every age, a terrible threat looms over Elliot and Faie’s adventure.

—Age of Safekeeping: The Present

—Age of Reconstruction: The Dark Ages

—Age of Magic: A Golden Age

—Age of Budding: The Birth of Civilization

■ Age of Reconstruction

Further into the past from Elliot’s home Age of Safekeeping is the Age of Reconstruction. Amidst the ruins of a more prosperous time, humanity’s remnants live on the brink of extinction.

There is no spell of Safekeeping in this age to repel the beastmen. Humans cling together in a village called Littlehope, eking out an existence with meager resources and defenses. Though most of the villagers have given in to despair, one young man with a glimmer of hope in his heart looks to the future.

■ Characters

Heurich (voiced by Daniel Sean Smith in English, Nobuhiko Okamoto in Japanese)

An adventurer from the village of Littlehope. He finds a kindred spirit in Elliot after their chance encounter, and shares with our hero information about the ruins and treasures discovered during his own adventures. While traveling the continent in search of a cure for his childhood friend Diona’s illness, Heurich hopes the power and wisdom gained on his journeys will be the key to pulling the world out of its dark ages.

Diona (voiced by Katrina Salisbury in English, Haruka Tomatsu in Japanese)

A young woman who grew up alongside Heurich in Littlehope. A chronic illness keeps her indoors most days. Her caring nature has earned her a warm place in many a heart of the other villagers. She constantly frets about Heurich’s reckless adventuring, so much so that their conversations often lead to quarrels.

Heurich (voiced by Rebecca Wang in English, Chiwa Saito in Japanese)

Littlehope’s resident historian and mutual friend of Heurich and Diona. Despite the world’s desperate condition, her passion for learning about humanity’s past drives her research. She takes an interest in Elliot and his similarities to Heurich, and offers him her expert advice.

■ System: Accessories

In addition to weapons and magicite, Elliot can equip and change accessories at any time from the equipment menu. Accessories are imbued with various effects beneficial to adventuring and should be selected in accordance with the demands of the ruins or foes Elliot currently faces.

—Equip the discerning monocle to double the window acceptable for a precise guard. It’s the prefect accessory for countering enemies that attack from a distance or bosses with an overbearing offense.

—The defalling charm protects Elliot from damage taken when falling into pits and lava—welcome insurance to have when navigating narrow footing and floating platforms.

■ System: Quests

In each age are people who need Elliot’s help. Hear them out and undertake the quests they give. There are some items that can only be obtained by fulfilling their requests.

—Begin a quest by speaking to those with a quest icon above their heads. Quests may involve anything from gathering items to fighting beastmen and more.

—Story progress may render some quests unable to be completed. When such an alert is displayed, make it a point to clear those quests before progressing further.

■ System: Shrines of Life

Mysterious structures known as shrines of life dot the map. Inside of them, players’ skills and intellect are tested, with special rewards awaiting those capable of overcoming the trials.

—A shrine of life always has an adventuring guidepost at its entrance, so you can quickly try again when things don’t go as planned.

—Trials include both combat-related and puzzle-solving challenges. Clearing a trial awards a shrine of life, which restores Elliot’s vitality. Collect four shards of life to increase Elliot’s max vitality by one.

■ System: Bonus Drops

Defeating enemies in succession without taking damage will trigger a bonus in which the defeated drop more loot. Taking damage will terminate the bonus.

—Bonus time is your chance to rake in the tul! Trounce as many foes as you can to fund your further adventures.

—In addition to tul, bonus drops increase the likelihood of finding arrows, bombs, and items that raise attack and defense. Continuing to defeat enemies during the bonus will raise the bonus level and the item drop rate in kind.

■ Adventuring Diversion: Cat Collecting

No matter the era, Philabieldia is filled with cats. If Elliot finds one, he can take it under his wing to bring it to a cat-fancying traveler in town who will reward Elliot for the number of cats he’s collected. Afterward, Elliot can care for and play with any cats he’s brought back.

—If you hear a cat’s meow during your adventuring, be sure to take a look around. Pay attention to any help Faie may have to offer, too.

—Rewards include all sorts of helpful items, such as those to help find more cats or increase the number of arrows and bombs Elliot can carry. Don’t take a catnap on cat collecting!

—You can feed cats you’ve brought back to town, from a selection of fish, meat, and cheese. Different cats have different tastes, and filling them up on the flavors they prefer will make them like you more.

—Once a cat has sufficiently warmed up to you, you can invite it to the orphanage to play with cat toys. Visit the item shop for all your pet care needs.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/the-adventures-of-elliot-the-millennium-tales-details-age-of-reconstruction
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: MONGIL: STAR DIVE launches April 15 for PC, iOS, and Android, later for PS5 and Xbox Series
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Smartphone
  • Xbox Series

New gameplay trailer and developer commentary released.

Action RPG MONGIL: STAR DIVE will launch for PC via the official launcher and Epic Games Store, iOS, and Android on April 15, followed by PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series at a later date, publisher Netmarble and developer Netmarble Monster announced. Pre-registration is available now via App Store, Google Play, and the official website.

“We are focusing all of our efforts on final polishing to deliver MONGIL: STAR DIVE at the highest level of quality,” said Netmarble Monster CEO Ken Kim in a press release. “We will repay the anticipation of players around the world with a game that meets their expectations.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Netmarble:

>MONGIL: STAR DIVE is the long-awaited sequel to the beloved 2013 mobile collectible RPG Monster Taming. Built with Unreal Engine 5, the title delivers high-quality cinematic storytelling, dynamic real-time combat, and a three-character tag battle system that emphasizes fast-paced action and strategic team switching.

The game features a cast of charming and distinctive characters alongside its signature “Monsterling Collection” system, which allows players to capture, collect, and synthesize monsters as companions. These Monsterlings can be equipped and combined to enhance combat performance and deepen progression strategy.

Ahead of launch, MONGIL: STAR DIVE has undergone multiple global closed beta tests to refine gameplay quality and stability. The game has also been showcased at major international gaming events including Gamescom and Tokyo Game Show, where players and media experienced hands-on demonstrations and live presentations.

At G-STAR 2025, the team drew attention by unveiling a new Korean-inspired region and characters for the first time. During GDC 2026, which is taking place in San Francisco this week, Netmarble has attracted global media attention through a collaborative on-site demo experience with Xbox and the release of new playthrough footage.

Watch a new gameplay trailer and developer presentation below.

Gameplay Trailer

English

Japanese

Korean

Release Date Reveal: Developer Commentary

English

Japanese

Korean



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/mongil-star-dive-launches-april-15-for-pc-ios-and-android-later-for-ps5-and-xbox-series

item.title: Powerful Pro Baseball 2026-2027 opening movie
item.content:encoded:
• PS4
  • Switch

Featuring the theme song "Highlight" sung by Anko.

Konami has released the opening movie for Powerful Pro Baseball 2026-2027, featuring the theme song “Highlight” sung by Anko.

Powerful Pro Baseball 2026-2027 is due out for PlayStation 4 and Switch on June 11 in Japan. Read more about the game here.

Watch the opening movie below.

Opening Movie



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/powerful-pro-baseball-2026-2027-opening-movie
